subject: Mathematics
title: What Is a Fractal?
description: A 60-second lesson on What Is a Fractal?.

hook.text: Zoom into a fractal and the same pattern reappears at every scale—forever.

buildup.text: A fractal is a shape where each part looks like a smaller copy of the whole.

discovery.text: Benoit Mandelbrot coined the term in 1975, from the Latin 'fractus' meaning broken.

twist.text: Fractals have fractional dimensions. A fractal curve can be 1.26-dimensional—between a line and a plane.

climax.text: Clouds, mountains, and coastlines are fractal-like. Nature uses self-similarity everywhere.

punchline.text: Infinite complexity from a simple rule, repeated at every scale.
